event_text: Norman power from France reshapes England after the Norman Conquest.
Philip II becomes king and strengthens Capetian royal authority.
France defeats a coalition at the Battle of Bouvines.
Louis IX becomes king of France.
Philip IV summons the Estates-General.
The Knights Templar are arrested in France by order of Philip IV.
Philip VI confiscates Gascony, helping begin the Hundred Years War between France and England.
The Black Death reaches France.
French forces are defeated at Agincourt.
Joan of Arc helps lift the Siege of Orléans.
Joan of Arc is executed at Rouen.
The Hundred Years War ends with French victory at Castillon.
Charles VIII invades Italy, beginning the Italian Wars.
Francis I wins the Battle of Marignano.
The Ordinance of Villers-Cotterêts makes French the language of legal administration.
The massacre of Vassy helps trigger the French Wars of Religion.
The St Bartholomew’s Day massacre kills many Huguenots.
Henry IV becomes king of France.
The Edict of Nantes grants limited toleration to Huguenots.
Henry IV is assassinated and Louis XIII becomes king.
France enters the Thirty Years War.
Louis XIV becomes king as a child.
The uprising known as the Fronde begins in Paris and challenges royal authority.
Louis XIV moves the royal court to Versailles.
The Edict of Nantes is revoked.
The War of the Spanish Succession begins, drawing France into a major European conflict.
Louis XIV dies after a long reign.
France fights Britain in the Seven Years War.
The Treaty of Paris ends most French rule in North America.
The French Revolution begins with the meeting of the Estates-General and the fall of the Bastille.
The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen is adopted.
The French monarchy is abolished and the First Republic is proclaimed.
Louis XVI is executed.
Napoleon Bonaparte seizes power in the Coup of 18 Brumaire.
Napoleon crowns himself Emperor of the French.
Napoleon is defeated at Waterloo and the Bourbon Restoration resumes.
The July Revolution replaces Charles X with Louis-Philippe.
The February Revolution creates the Second Republic.
Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte becomes Emperor Napoleon III.
France is defeated in the Franco-Prussian War.
The Paris Commune is suppressed.
Free, secular primary education is expanded under Jules Ferry laws.
The Eiffel Tower opens for the Exposition Universelle.
The Dreyfus Affair divides French society.
The law on separation of churches and state is passed.
France fights in the First World War.
The Battle of Verdun becomes one of the war’s longest battles.
The Treaty of Versailles is signed near Paris.
The Popular Front government introduces major social reforms.
Germany defeats France and the Vichy regime is established.
Paris is liberated from German occupation.
The Fourth Republic is established.
The Algerian War brings political crisis and decolonisation.
Charles de Gaulle returns to power and the Fifth Republic is founded.
The Évian Accords end the Algerian War.
May 1968 protests and strikes disrupt France.
Valéry Giscard d’Estaing becomes president.
François Mitterrand becomes president.
France celebrates the bicentenary of the French Revolution.
France narrowly approves the Maastricht Treaty by referendum.
France wins the FIFA World Cup on home soil.
Euro banknotes and coins enter circulation in France.
Suburban riots spread after the deaths of two teenagers near Paris.
Terrorist attacks strike Charlie Hebdo and later Paris.
Emmanuel Macron is elected president.
The Yellow Vests movement begins.
The COVID-19 pandemic brings lockdowns and major disruption to France.
